name: agent-index-core
description: Core infrastructure for the agent-index system. v3.22.7 (Release C.1.3.7 — agent-index-first enforcement): connectorbypass — the CLAUDE.md Agent-Index First rule now explicitly states that a query naming an agent-index collection ('our library', 'our projects') MUST route to that collection's capability (read via aifs), never a same-scope external connector — INCLUDING the org's own storage backend connector (Google Drive / OneDrive / SharePoint); reading org content from the raw connector bypasses the catalog/ACL/pointer model and yields wrong conclusions (an unshared doc read as 'deleted', a private doc's title-only pointer read as 'broken'). Closes bug 20260706-8d20ea22-connectorbypass. Pairs with gdrive adapter 2.8.2 (aifs_search returns resolvable id: locators) + library 1.2.0 (catalog/ACL reconcile). v3.22.6 (Release C.1.3.6 — member onboarding + hygiene): memberauthbootstrap — a first-time member with a backend-configured agent-index.json is routed to member-bootstrap (NOT @ai:setup/org-setup), and the local CLAUDE.md bootstrap protocol now carries the OAuth start→complete flow so a member's agent can authenticate before it can read remote skills; pairs with gdrive adapter 2.8.1 (auth_code infers the complete action + a self-documenting start message, ported from onedrive, which already had both). staletmpinject — standards.md forbids consuming a cross-session /tmp scratch file you did not create (use a unique mktemp path + verify the populate succeeded), the fix for a phantom /members-registry.json entry injected from a stale temp during invite-member. upload-install-log secret scan now matches secret VALUES, not bare token names, so descriptive prose mentioning client_secret no longer false-positives. v3.22.5 (Release C.1.3.5 — mitmcadefer): apply-updates Phase 3 (adapter bundle) no longer defers the bundle update on the expected `[aifs] Proxy detected` sandbox-proxy CA notice — that notice is documented, benign transport behavior and is not a trust signal. Bundle integrity is now codified as required Phase 3 steps: a SHA-256 checksum gate (fetched bundle vs the backend `adapter.json` `exec_bundle_checksum`), an embedded-URL/domain scan against `required_domains`, and a post-install `aifs_auth_status` smoke test — all fail-loud. Only the unwarranted deferral is removed; the verification stays (closes bug 20260630-8d20ea22-mitmcadefer, low). Pairs with gdrive adapter 2.8.0 (M2 durable committed-size read-back — gdrive-arm parity). v3.22.4 (Release C.1.3.4 — admin-path write & source integrity, from ms_install_10): M1 adminupstreamstale — admin 'update our org'/upstream/repair source-of-truth is the local git clones, never WebSearch or the member-facing public directory (a web-searched stale snapshot concluded 'nothing to update' while the org was ahead); publish-updates/check-updates/standards updated (CLAUDE.md template too). M2 collectionjson-tornwrite — onedrive adapter 2.5.0 write() does a durable committed-size read-back (re-reads the committed object size, not the PUT response's item.size) so a torn/partial commit on a non-sentinel JSON can't ship undetected (core collection.json shipped 31030/32402 on ms_install_10); create-org Step 9 + publish-updates Step 0 mandate the same durable read-back on every bulk upload; JSON config carries a _file_end sentinel. M3 — edit-org sources the adapter from the local clone and deploys text files from the git blob (CRLF fix) with a smoke-test; publish-updates Step 0 adds a pre-publish version-mismatch guard. M4 scriptnulltail — orchestrator scripts authored via heredoc-to-native + compile-check, never the Write tool onto the mount. Pairs with onedrive adapter 2.5.0. v3.22.3 (Release C.1.3.3 — fresh-install version-truth + welcome-email link, from ms_install_10): K1 createorgversionstale — create-org 3.9.1 now writes `agent-index.json` `version` from the ACTUAL installed core `collection.json` version at write time (was left at the template's stale hardcoded 3.1.1 on every fresh org, since apply-updates' core-update — the only corrector — never fires on a brand-new org). K2 migration5wrongdir — apply-updates 3.13.3 corrects Migration 5's direction: reconcile BOTH `agent-index.json.version` and member-index `agent_index_version` to the authoritative `org-config.agent_index_version`, never blind-copy one local field onto the other (the 3.22.1 version would have downgraded a correct member-index to the stale 3.1.1 on a fresh org). K3 bootstraplinkunavailable — invite-member 1.11.1 builds the welcome-email bootstrap link from `stat`'s new `web_url` field (onedrive adapter 2.4.0 / gdrive 2.7.0 return it; the adapter previously exposed no webUrl/createLink op so the welcome email had to be skipped on ms_install_10), with an explicit admin-paste fallback when absent — never a bare path. Validated on a fresh OneDrive org (ms_install_10): create-org/collsetupgap/adminregidentity/invite/onboard/crossdriveread(open)/unshare/transfer(NOT_IMPLEMENTED) all green. Pairs with onedrive adapter 2.4.0 + gdrive 2.7.0. v3.22.2 (Release C.1.3.2 — admin-rollout hardening + the I1 regression fix): J0 — apply-updates 3.13.2 reads collection files **path-primary (`/{collection}`) with `id:{folder_id}` fallback**, fixing the C.1.3 regression where bare `id:{folder_id}` resolved against the member's OWN drive (`/me/drive`) instead of the SharePoint site drive, so OneDrive members couldn't receive collection updates (`i1onedrivebreak`); org-setup already used the path read, so onboarding was unaffected. J4 — apply-updates retries transient `aifs_read` failures (≤3×) distinct from the CONFIG_ERROR hard-abort (`aifsreadflaky`). J5 — permission-change-helper 1.3.2 hands a shell-aware ABSOLUTE-path `--cli` command (PowerShell `&` call operator, no `&&`; canonical binary name) (`clihelppwsh`/`clihelpcwd`). J2 — clone-script-generator emits a resilient infra-clone (clear `index.lock` + `reset --hard` before checkout; continue past a single repo's failure with a per-repo summary) (`clonescriptdirty`). J1 — repos carry a `.gitattributes` (`* text=auto eol=lf`) so Windows autocrlf can't dirty the working tree (which blocked `git checkout`) or corrupt the byte-exact bundle (`crlfcheckout`). J3 — standards: host byte-exact deploy via `git cat-file blob <tag>:…`, never a working-tree copy; a truncated-executor read is a stale Cowork mount → relaunch first, not corruption (`adapterdeploydoc`/`stalemountexec`). No adapter/binary runtime change. v3.22.1 (Release C.1.3 patch — low-priority cleanup): permission-change-helper 1.3.1 hands the `--cli` fallback a cwd-independent command (`cd <project_dir> && …` or an absolute spec path) so a member running from the wrong directory no longer hits 'No such file or directory' (`clihelpcwd`). apply-updates 3.13.1 adds standing Migration 5 syncing member-index `agent_index_version` to agent-index.json `version` (`versionfielddrift`, observed 3.21.0 vs 3.20.0). standards.md: agent-side git in the sandbox is read-only via `git show <ref>:<path>` — never checkout/switch/add, which take index.lock and tear files over the mount (`gitwritelock`). `marketplacestaleraw` needs no code change — the admin upstream check is already git-based and members read the backend manifest (Release C). No adapter/binary change. v3.22.0 (Release C.1.3 — distribution integrity + cross-drive read): the backend-distribution loop is now real end-to-end. publish-updates 3.9.0 republishes /shared/dist/ on every publish (Step 6.5 — closes publishdistgap, the manifest went stale after in-place updates) with a publish-time round-trip SHA self-check. apply-updates 3.13.0 + check-updates 2.10.0 now ACTUALLY compute + verify dist artifact SHAs against the manifest and refuse mismatches (shagateunimplemented — the gate was promised but never ran); backend-distribution.md mandates a Canonical SHA-256 rule (hash the stored `size` bytes, never aifs_read stdout — the 412094b4-vs-e1d549e4 manifestsha trailing-newline bug). apply-updates reads collection files via id:{folder_id} ONLY — the legacy /{collection} silent fallback is removed (memberapplynotdistaware, Option A: one read path), and a new config-validity gate fails fast on CONFIG_ERROR and never persists error payloads (applyerrorpollution — the 38-manifest pollution from a mount-tear). org-setup 3.6.0 presents permission-helper registration as a do-it-now/verified step on the 'set up my workspace' path (helpernoreg — the path that previously buried it as an optional footnote). standards.md adds 'reads go through aifs only' (wrongconnectorfallback) + cross-drive ID anchors. Pairs with onedrive adapter 2.3.0 (crossdriveread): a member can now OPEN content another member shared from their personal OneDrive via the id:{driveId}:{itemId} anchor (item IDs are drive-scoped; a bare anchor 404'd cross-drive). No new Entra scope. Gates customer B. v3.21.0 (Release C.1.2 — ms-install-9 collsetupgap): create-org 3.9.0 adds Step 9.5 — after cloning the Phase-1-selected collections, it now UPLOADS **and provisions** each one (the install-collection Steps 4–5.7 flow: setup interview/defaults → collection-setup-responses.md with setup_status:complete → seed files → collaborative-acls + code-dir reader grant via the sanctioned bootstrap direct path + helper fallback → provider registration → status:installed). Pre-C.1.2 the two-script flow raw-uploaded collections without provisioning, so org-setup hard-blocked every member (and the admin) from installing their capabilities until a manual recovery (ms-install-9). Surfaces bug-reports admin_roles dependency on org roles. Validated: full clean OneDrive install (ms-install-9) passed first-try on C.1.1 (singletag/colltags/binfield/tls12/sharedocbug/corebin/loggap all held). Helper binary unchanged (0.6.0, unsigned-bypass); no rebuild. v3.20.0 (Release C.1.1 — ms-install-8 hardening): fixes the generated-clone-script bugs the admin had to patch live during the first full OneDrive install. clone-script-generator: single-tag repos resolve correctly (force array context — a one-tag repo no longer indexes to the char '1'); collections clone the default branch + pin HEAD-SHA (they ship from main with no release tags) while infra stays tag-pinned; binary version reads `current_version` (not `version`) and the script forces TLS 1.2 before download (PS 5.1 defaults to TLS 1.0, which GitHub's CDN rejects). create-org 3.8.0: aifs_share args corrected to path/subject/role (not resource/recipient); Step 9 excludes binaries/build-artifacts from the core upload; large binaries upload in a single foreground call (never backgrounded). corebin: removed the committed 9 MB bin/agent-index-show-plan.exe + added a .gitignore (bin/, **/dist/, *.exe, *.app) so build output never bloats core or the org backend. standards.md: documents create-org install-time bootstrap as the one sanctioned exception to the helper-gated permission model (org creator owns the whole tenant at creation). Helper binary unchanged (0.6.0, unsigned-bypass); no rebuild. v3.19.0 (Release C.1 — GitHub-free install orchestration + signed cross-platform helper): create-org makes ZERO GitHub calls — all GitHub access lives in two host-run scripts (clone-script-generator infra mode: ls-remote tag discovery with NO main fallback + backend-matched signed-binary download from the freshly-cloned listings; then a collection-selection interview reading the local catalog; then collections mode). Fixes ms-install-7 — wrong-backend binary (binwrongbackend), stale version pins (staleversionpins), silent main fallback (tagnofallback), PowerShell script failures (clonescriptps1: exit-code-not-stderr, no apostrophes, ExecutionPolicy Bypass), large-binary mount-stale publish (binmountstale: host-hash-gated), never-asked-collections, loggap-on-resume. apply-updates 3.12.0 + member-bootstrap 3.7.0: per-platform binary install (darwin installs the notarized .app, never --register on a bare binary), native-platform register failure is a HARD error with a verify step. Helper binary 0.6.0 is code-signed (Windows Authenticode/Trusted Signing; macOS Developer ID + notarized .app; optional Linux GPG) — fixes Smart App Control hard-block (bug 20260626-8d20ea22-2) and macOS .app registration (bug 20260626-8d20ea22); build-all.sh signs before checksums with a verify-signed gate (lib/permission-helper-go/SIGNING.md). Gates customer B (supersedes C). v3.18.0 (Release C — org-backend distribution): members never fetch from github.com — each org's backend is its distribution layer. The admin (the only GitHub touch, over git clone/pull, not the rate-limited raw/REST API) publishes collections + directories + the helper binary to /shared/dist/; members read everything from there, SHA-verified against /shared/dist/manifest.json (the org version authority). New subroutines clone-script-generator (recurring tag-pinned clone/pull+binary script the admin runs) and backend-distribution (publish/verify + member read contract). create-org generates the clone script + publishes /shared/dist/ + bakes the binary into the bootstrap zip; apply-updates/check-updates/member-bootstrap read directory+binary from the backend (member-current = vs manifest; org-vs-upstream = admin git check); download-collection sources from the local clone. standards.md leads with the backend-first model; the SHA-pinned GitHub fetch is demoted to admin-only/deprecated fallback (removal targeted next release). Eliminates the stale-cache + GitHub rate-limit class (Jeff/ms-install-5/ms-install-6) and absorbs the version-check accuracy work (listinglag/shasolve). Adapter unchanged (2.2.1). v3.17.0 (Release B.3 — group-permission/access-model cleanup): catbredundant — invite-member no longer applies per-member 'Category B' reader shares; a member reads + enumerates /shared, collection roots, and root files via all-members GROUP membership (create-org Step 4.5 now also grants the group reader on /shared — the missing piece), reaching its own space by ID-anchor. Validated on gdrive with a group-only member (list + read both work). One per-member grant remains: the artifact-dir writer (needed on gdrive, skipped on OneDrive). recipidform — share recipient is the resolved UPN/mail, never the objectId (helper rejects GUIDs). versionmarker — create-org + member-bootstrap stamp the ACTUAL installed core version (was hardcoded 2.0.0). member-bootstrap writes member-index shell-first and documents the group-membership prerequisite + group->library propagation latency (ms-install-5's 'core invisible' was propagation, not a cache — pathcachestale; adapter caches no negatives, no change). standards.md Addressing clarified to group-conveyed enumeration. Adapter UNCHANGED (2.2.1); marketplace UNCHANGED. Deferred to their own work: nameambig (id-anchor collection-root resolution — cross-backend), version-check accuracy (listinglag/shasolve2). v3.16.0 (Release B.2 — ms-install-5 hardening): identityperm — create-org now requests the Graph `User.Read.All` delegated permission (+ admin consent) the identity resolver needs (plain User.Read can't read other users, so the B.1 resolver 403'd on every live lookup); invite-member 1.9.0 keeps member_hash from the roster email but resolves a separate grantable `sharing_identity`, accepting an admin-supplied tenant UPN/objectId when the roster domain isn't a verified tenant domain (the common case), and branches ACCESS_DENIED (consent gap — member exists) vs INVALID_SUBJECT (genuine no-match) instead of conflating them. accessmodel — invite-member documents the controlled direct-shares-only test path. Reliability: localcfgtrunc (write local config shell-first, not just verify-after-write), resume/idempotency (Step 3c skip-guard when already complete), loggap (re-open the install log as the first resume action). adapterdirstale/sharesolve (create-org fetches the adapter directory via the SHA-pinned Distribution fetch protocol — the bare URL served a stale 1.0.0 directory in ms-install-5 — and treats the downloaded bundle's adapter.json as authoritative for version). Pairs with onedrive adapter 2.2.1. v3.15.0 (Release B.1): ms-install-4 hardening — identitymap (invite-member 1.8.0 resolves + persists sharing_identity via the new aifs_resolve_identity adapter op; sharing tasks read it), helperbypass (no direct-apply fallback, even from a sandbox), nohelperpin (create-org + member-bootstrap install the pinned helper), admincaps/manualinvite (create-org installs admin caps + points to @ai:invite-member), hostregister (manual host --register messaging), pkcerestart (sandbox-local verifier persistence + reuse + infer-complete), memberlicense, deadsyncpref removal, member-index verify-after-write. Pairs with onedrive adapter 2.2.0. v3.14.0: OneDrive member-onboarding — invite-member 1.7.0 is now backend-aware: it provisions a new member's access via direct per-member shares through the permission-change-helper (no SharePoint site pre-staging required), with the M365-group add as an out-of-band roster/site step (admin-attested). The non-site-member direct-share read+list behavior is the gdrive-parity assumption confirmed by the 2-account M365 test; fallback to group/site-membership documented. Also: create-org surfaces the member OneDrive-license prerequisite to the admin during onedrive setup (admin-facing half of memberlicense — admins control M365 licensing; includes how to assign a license). v3.13.0 (Release B — multi-member ACL / selective sharing for OneDrive): create-org now provisions all-members + collaborator access via the backend's all_members_group on BOTH backends (Google Group on gdrive, M365 group on onedrive) — onedrive 2.1.0 implements share/unshare/getPermissions/search so the per-file grants run instead of the A.1 manual-site-membership interim (closes bug 20260614-8d20ea22-spacl); the all_members_group is wired into the onedrive connection schema and captured at create-org. permission-change-helper documents the per-adapter binary: apply-updates installs the build matching remote_filesystem.backend (gdrive embeds Drive, onedrive embeds Graph) to the shared install path, so downstream refs are unchanged. Reliability bundle: localcfgtrunc (verify-after-write for local config in create-org), pkcerestart (member-bootstrap must complete-not-restart auth; onedrive startAuth reuses a fresh PKCE verifier), setupresp (install-collection guardrail — defaults/bulk installs still write collection-setup-responses.md; org-setup surfaces an actionable per-collection remedy), memberlicense (onedrive distinguishes no-license from not-signed-in; member-bootstrap relays it + prereq). trunc doc-truncation audit closed. v3.9.1: raw-URL normalization (closes the remaining half of the fetch-cache bug class, finding F7) — the agent-index.json template and a new apply-updates Step 1.5 Migration 3 rewrite refs/heads/main raw.githubusercontent URLs to the /main/ short-form; the fetch layer strips query params on the refs/heads form and serves long-stale bytes (observed weeks-old directories despite cache-busters), silently breaking pin validation and binary sha verification. Idempotent, heals every install on next @ai:update. v3.9.0: member spaces move to members' own My Drive (owner-sovereign sharing; closes finding F12 — Shared-Drive folders can only be shared by drive Managers, so non-admin owners could never apply owned-content grants). member-bootstrap 3.2.0 defines the ensure-my-drive-space subroutine (Agent-Index-Private at id:root, resolved-ID rule, content migration from legacy /members/ spaces, handshake file); org-setup 3.5.0 and apply-updates 3.9.0 (Step 1.5 Migration 2) reuse it — existing members migrate automatically on next @ai:update; invite-member 1.6.0 stops creating member folders (registry member_folder_id null until reconcile); publish-updates 3.7.0 Step 6d reconciles handshakes into the registry every run; remove-member 1.2.0 adds the mandatory departure acknowledgment (owner shares SURVIVE removal — org loses governance, not recipient access; pointers annotated owner_departed; adoption path surfaced) and narrows grant revocation to the artifacts dir (+ legacy member dir if present). standards.md Addressing updated (custody/cooperation note; soft-delete nuance). Also retro-documents permission-helper-go 0.4.0 (bare id:{folderId} spec resources + browser-visible fatal errors; bug 20260604-8d20ea22-164642-e046). v3.8.1: apply-updates 3.8.1 adds Step 1.5 member-state self-heal — standing idempotent migrations that run on every @ai:update even when no entries are pending. Migration 1 caches member_folder_id from members-registry.json into member-index.json for existing members (the 3.8.0 ensure-cached logic in org-setup/member-bootstrap only reaches new members; post-release finding F3, owned-content release). v3.8.0: ID-anchored addressing & member-folder identity — standards.md addressing/soft-delete/pointer-index conventions; invite-member 1.5.0 records member_folder_id in members-registry.json at invite time; org-setup 3.4.0 + member-bootstrap 3.1.0 cache it into member-index.json; companion to gdrive adapter 2.5.0 (id:{folderId}/ anchors, stat returns id). Admin backfill required for pre-3.8.0 members (see CHANGELOG). v3.7.6: cascade-cleanup release closing the four-bug cluster discovered during 3.7.4 verification. (1) permission-helper-go 0.2.0 → 0.3.0 — binary now accepts v1.1 spec format and threads inherit:false through to Drive's InheritedPermissionsDisabled (the entire feature shipped in core 3.7.3 had never executed in production); also writes an atomic outcome JSON file alongside the spec (closes bugs 20260527-8d20ea22 + 20260527-8d20ea22-2). (2) org-setup 3.3.0 → 3.3.1 — corrects the canonical setup-responses path at two call sites (closes the spec half of bug 20260527-8d20ea22-4). (3) apply-updates 3.7.0 → 3.7.1 — Phase 4.5 manifest-sync subroutine extended with step 9 (org-mandated values reconciliation); CURRENT_SUBROUTINE_REVISION bumped 2→3 so the sweep fires on every existing install (closes the data half of bug 20260527-8d20ea22-4). (4) create-org 3.1.0 → 3.1.1 — new Step 4.5 grants all@{domain} reader on /CLAUDE.md, /org-config.json, /members-registry.json at install time (closes the spec half of bug 20260527-8d20ea22-3). (5) invite-member 1.3.0 → 1.4.0 — Category B share-set extended with the same three root-level org-readable files so new members on existing orgs get the grants in their onboarding spec. v3.7.5: companion follow-up to 3.7.4 — invite-member 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 ships Category B direct-share grants (closes the gap in bug 20260522-8d20ea22 that 3.7.4 left open). v3.7.2: cleanup patch — apply-updates 3.5.0 syncs agent-index.json adapter_version from adapter.json during Phase 3 (closes bundle-vs-config-adapter-drift idea); remove-member 1.1.0 revokes the explicit ACLs invite-member granted via permission-helper (closes bug 20260513-8d20ea22-3); view-audit 1.1.0 surfaces working folder URLs + admin audit URLs instead of dead Drive Activity URLs (closes bug 20260513-8d20ea22-2); permission-helper validate.js cleaned of trailing debris (republished via Step 0 sync). Companion: marketplace 2.5.0 adds contract-aware surfacing; developer 1.3.1 adds JS-integrity heuristic to preflight-cli that catches the debris class. v3.7.1: trigger-fix patch. (1) Phase 4.5 sentinel — adds a CURRENT_SUBROUTINE_REVISION constant + manifest_sync_subroutine_revision[] tracking field so future subroutine-step additions automatically re-fire on existing installs (closes bug 20260512-8d20ea22 — the 3.7.0 step 7 didn’t trigger on installs with pre-populated manifest_sync). (2) publish-updates writes back to org-config.installed_collections[] after each successful publish — fixes the longstanding drift where infrastructure version bumps never advanced these entries (closes bug 20260512-8d20ea22-2). apply-updates 3.4.0 -> 3.4.1; publish-updates 3.3.0 -> 3.4.0. v3.7.0: release-hygiene + admin-UX cleanup release. Five items: (1) apply-updates.md Edge Cases section restored from long-standing truncation; (2) member-index installed[].version drift fix — org-setup spec clarified, Phase 4.5 subroutine extended to reconcile data on next sync; (3) check-updates Available-to-Install section (in marketplace 2.4.0); (4) admin disambiguation prompt for ambiguous "check for updates" routing; (5) preflight CLI in developer 1.3.0 for release-script-runs-preflight integration. apply-updates 3.3.1 -> 3.4.0; org-setup 3.2.1 -> 3.2.2. v3.6.1: fix-up release — apply-updates 3.3.1 hoists the manifest_sync drift-detection sweep out of Phase 4's per-collection-update loop into a new unconditional Phase 4.5. Closes bug 20260511-8d20ea22 (3.6.0's Phase 4 step 3.5 was structurally unreachable when an apply-updates batch contained no collection-update operations — including the very batch that shipped 3.6.0 itself; the manifest_sync backfill promised in 3.6.0's CHANGELOG could not actually run on the upgrade that introduced the feature). The file-content sync mechanics are extracted into a standalone subroutine called from both Phase 4 step 3.5 (per-collection-update) and Phase 4.5 (unconditional sweep). v3.6.0: bug-cleanup release — apply-updates Phase 4 file-content sync (rewrites local .md/setup/manifest files during collection-update; closes bugs 20260502-8d20ea22-5 and 20260507-8d20ea22, with granular per-collection manifest_sync tracking in member-index.json); apply-updates Phase 1 step 5 extended to also strip the misplaced `exec` block from org-config.json (alongside the existing mcp_server strip); publish-updates 3.3.0 — Step 0 source-tree skip-list applied symmetrically across upload AND delete decisions plus new ephemeral-file patterns; workspace-marker-vs-collection-template Phase 2 — legacy collection-template file retired and apply-updates fallback removed; create-org template + org-config-schema.json no longer emit the duplicate exec block; gdrive credentials inline-vs-split rationale documented in schema and authoring guide. v3.5.0: publish-updates 3.2.0 with `--check-upstream` and smart prerequisite detection. v3.4.0: native Go permission-helper binary v0.2.0 + binary-distribution registry. v3.3.x: permission-change-helper skill + invite-member rewrite. v3.2.0: org-setup management dashboard. v3.1.x: extended adapter contract v2.0. v3.0.x: natural language triggers.
author: agent-index

triggers.description: Run first-time member onboarding and capability installation
Install or reinstall available collections
Initialize agent-index member workspace
View or modify session preferences and settings
Adjust member configuration options
Change session behavior settings
Customize natural language trigger mappings
Get a guided tour of the agent-index system
Walk through agent-index features and concepts
Learn about available agent-index capabilities
Set or clear the org's pinned version for a registered binary tool
Roll back the permission-helper or other binary tool to a prior version
Pin the org to a specific version of a binary tool
Onboard a new agent-index member to this org
Add a new member to the org
Invite a new member by email
Run the new-member onboarding flow
Remove a member from the agent-index roster
Take a departing member out of the registry
Remove a member entry from the registry
Remove a member when they leave
Show who has access to a resource
Display the permission list for a resource
List members and groups with access to a resource
Inspect ACLs on a resource
Open the audit trail for a resource
Show recent changes and who made them
Surface activity history for a resource
See the activity stream on a resource
Run the workspace policy diagnostic
Confirm Workspace settings support the access-control model
Verify the org's permission configuration
Check and apply pending org updates
Apply the latest org changes to local install
Check if there are pending updates to apply
Build a new agent-index collection from scratch
Author a new collection with guided workflow

Check a collection for standards compliance
Run validation checks on a collection
Test reachability of all required hosts in the canonical network allowlist
Test which required hosts are reachable from this Cowork network
Diagnose whether the network allowlist supports agent-index operations
Run the canonical reachability check for agent-index
